message:
I'll never forget last spring when a water main broke two blocks away and the city issued a boil-water advisory for our whole neighborhood. My first instinct was panic—then I remembered we'd actually filled a few extra jugs a couple months back, almost as an afterthought.

We weren't scrambling to the store with everyone else. We weren't stressed about drinking water for a few days. That small decision—just keeping some clean water on hand—turned what could've been annoying into "oh, we're totally fine."

It's wild how a tiny bit of setup takes zero stress out of situations you can't control. That's what I'm actually after with all this—not doomsday thinking, just the peace of mind that comes with small, practical moves.

What's one small prep thing you've done that actually came in handy when you needed it? I'd love to hear.
